summary: The suite covers all six public functions and hits most task requirements: INI parsing (sections, comments, multi-line, blank lines, empty values), type coercion (bool/int/float/string), schema required-key validation, JSON output, YAML output (quoting rules), and an end-to-end integration test with fixture files. The main coverage gap is the 'value types' dimension of schema validation — the schema DSL only exercises Required keys; no test checks that a schema-declared type (e.g., port must be integer) is enforced or rejected. Rigor is moderate: inline comments, backslash continuation, empty files, and YAML special characters are tested, but important edge cases are absent: malformed section headers (missing ']'), duplicate keys in the same section, keys containing '=' in the value, and what happens when the INI file cannot be read (file-not-found path is never exercised). Design is adequate but has three recurring weaknesses: (1) BeforeAll { Import-Module } is copied verbatim into every Describe block instead of being lifted into a top-level BeforeAll or a shared helper; (2) the seven Invoke-TypeCoercion tests are structurally identical and would benefit from a -TestCases / ForEach data-driven pattern; (3) no Mocks are used anywhere despite the task explicitly asking for them, which means the module boundary is never isolated — the end-to-end tests would silently re-test parser bugs rather than isolating the migration orchestration logic. The AfterAll cleanup and use of $script: scoping in the integration Describe are well-done.
